column27:
Need a testing apparatus for the new trap DIR prototype. Bob is going to develop the testing method and the criteria that needs to be meet. While I will develop the apparatus for the testing based on those criteria. So far I think the following is needed.

* IR Camera (Qualitative measurement)
* Power Draw (Quantitative measurement)
* Deception Ability (Qualitative measurement)
* Apparatus to hold tarp or object to put tarp over
* Computer (Rasp Pi or simple computer for data collection)

* Create document describing apparatus or testing
column64: 26/Mar/26 2:43 PM;712020:a91169b6-cfe5-4669-9aef-236f054c1307;I am going to have a meeting tomorrow with Bob and Tony going over the criteria for testing which will help with determine the apparatus for testing.

column64:
19/Feb/26 2:40 PM;712020:a91169b6-cfe5-4669-9aef-236f054c1307;!image-20260219-213906.png|width=650,alt="image-20260219-213906.png"!

90% there on the PCB design. Placing final components to board. Note that his is REV1 so the board is much larger than what we want to scaled down too. This is primarily due to using through-hole component and not surface mounted device which have much smaller footprints. REV2 will be a SMD design
column65: 26/Feb/26 2:53 PM;712020:a91169b6-cfe5-4669-9aef-236f054c1307;Trying to finalized the board on a single layer, but thinking we will have to do a two sides board, which it a little more of a process when it comes to manufacturing the cell.
column66: 17/Mar/26 1:55 PM;712020:a91169b6-cfe5-4669-9aef-236f054c1307;Got the final CNC pathing done. Need to update the drill depth and also increase the via hole size. For some reasons, these hole are way to small for header pins.
column68: 20/Mar/26 9:05 AM;712020:a91169b6-cfe5-4669-9aef-236f054c1307;Increased all the trace widths and spacing to make it easier to CNC and provide a bit more forgiveness when swapping it around to the back side.
column69: 26/Mar/26 2:01 PM;712020:a91169b6-cfe5-4669-9aef-236f054c1307;Current having an issue with the prototype. Everything works up until the push-pull circuit. I am going to cnc another board to see if that is my issue.
column70:
26/Mar/26 2:38 PM;712020:a91169b6-cfe5-4669-9aef-236f054c1307;Running down a bug in the PCB. For some reason the PP drive section is pulling current from somewhere. I have tested the entire circuit separately with the NPN and PNP, and those both work by themselves but not together. Kinda of a head scratcher. 

I have reviewed the bread board and KiCAD schematic and cannot figure out what is wrong with it. I will try one more time before CNC a new board and trying again.
column71: 27/Mar/26 3:56 PM;712020:a91169b6-cfe5-4669-9aef-236f054c1307;Found the issue. change the resistor on the push pull to 10K and that solved my issue. Going to go ahead with CNC the 4 pcbs and then use them on the 2x2 sample
